- Printed application form completed
duly online (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
or
manual form (file:///D:/VFS_GLOBAL_GIT/www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/philippines/france/english/passport-services/pdf/parc-manual-form-nov-2021.pdf)
duly completed.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Adult Applicants (18 Years old and above)
                                                                        
The ePassport Renewal Centre can assist any
                                                                            applicants wishing to renew their ePassports
                                                                            as long as there are no changes on the
                                                                            details of the biographical page. Passports
                                                                            issued to adults will be valid for 10 years.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For Adult Applicants (18 Years old and above)
                                                                        
The ePassport Renewal Centre can assist any
                                                                            applicants wishing to renew their ePassports
                                                                            as long as there are no changes on the
                                                                            details of the biographical page. Passports
                                                                            issued to adults will be valid for 10 years.
                                                                        

## Application Fees

[Table:  Adults]
Please Note:
- Fees for renewal of passport are payable
                                                                                by credit or debit card and are the same
                                                                                as the fee charged at the Embassy of the
                                                                                Philippines in France for ePassport
                                                                                renewal.
- Applicants shall be charged a service
                                                                                fee of 51 EUR (incl. VAT) per
                                                                                application in addition to the Passport
                                                                                fees payable by credit or debit card.
                                                                                The applicable fee in EUR is as per the
                                                                                current exchange rate. It is subject to
                                                                                change without notice due to sudden
                                                                                foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable

## Documents Required
- Applicant to come in person to the ePassport Renewal Centre on the date of appointment
- Printed application form completed
duly online (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
or
manual form (file:///D:/VFS_GLOBAL_GIT/www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/philippines/france/english/passport-services/pdf/parc-manual-form-nov-2021.pdf)
duly completed.
- Most recent original ePassport
- Photocopy of data page of most recent ePassport.

## Photo Specifications
Passport photograph will be captured at the
                                                                            ePassport Renewal Centre.
Important Reminders:
Eyeglasses, coloured contact lenses,
                                                                            jewellery (earring, necklace), heavy make-up
                                                                            should be removed before photo capturing. It
                                                                            is also advisable for applicants having sore
                                                                            eyes, eye sty or any redness in the eyes to
                                                                            come for e-passport renewal when their eye
                                                                            condition improves.

## Processing Time
The new passport may be released within 4-6 weeks from the date of submission of application, barring unforeseen events

## Application Form
To complete your online application form
                                                                            please
click
                                                                                here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
To complete a manual form please
click
                                                                                here

visa_types.name: Minor
visa_types.documents_required:
- Applicant to come in person to the ePassport Renewal Centre on the date of appointment (minors must be accompanied by a parent – see instruction below)
- Printed application form completed duly online or manual form duly completed.
- Most recent original ePassport
- Photocopy of data page of most recent ePassport.
- Original and photocopy of Birth Certificate issued by Philippines Statistics Authority or Original Report of Birth issued by Philippine Embassy in France (if born in France).
- Original and (1) photocopy of passport of accompanying parent
- Original PSA marriage contract of parents or Original Report of Marriage issued by the Philippine Embassy in France (if married in France) of parents, if available.
Important Instruction for applicant:
- Personal appearance of the child and either parent.
- If parents are not married, mother must accompany the minor for renewal.
Certificate of Marriage issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) or Report of Marriage issued by the Philippine Embassy in France (if married in France) is required when only the father files the application. (Original and photocopy)
Certificate of Live Birth/Report of Birth issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA). (Original and photocopy)
Passport of either parent with one (1) photocopy of data page
Additional requirements may be requested by the Embassy in compliance with relevant/prevailing rules and regulations as prescribed by the competent authority.
visa_types.processing_time:
The new passport may be released within 4-6 weeks
                                                                        from the date of submission of application,
                                                                        barring unforeseen events
visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Minor Applicants (below 18 years old)
Passport to be issued to a minor child will
                                                                            be valid for 5 years.
A minor applicant must be accompanied by
                                                                            either parent during the time of
                                                                            application.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For Minor Applicants (below 18 years old)
Passport to be issued to a minor child will
                                                                            be valid for 5 years.
A minor applicant must be accompanied by
                                                                            either parent during the time of
                                                                            application. 

## Application Fees

[Table:  Minor]
Please Note:
- Fees for renewal of passport are payable
                                                                                by credit or debit card and are the same
                                                                                as the fee charged at the Embassy of the
                                                                                Philippines in France for ePassport
                                                                                renewal.
- Applicants shall be charged a service
                                                                                fee of 51 EUR (incl. VAT) per
                                                                                application in addition to the Passport
                                                                                fees payable by credit or debit card.
                                                                                The applicable fee in EUR is as per the
                                                                                current exchange rate. It is subject to
                                                                                change without notice due to sudden
                                                                                foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable

## Documents Required
- Applicant to come in person to the ePassport Renewal Centre on the date of appointment (minors must be accompanied by a parent – see instruction below)
- Printed application form completed duly online or manual form duly completed.
- Most recent original ePassport
- Photocopy of data page of most recent ePassport.
- Original and photocopy of Birth Certificate issued by Philippines Statistics Authority or Original Report of Birth issued by Philippine Embassy in France (if born in France).
- Original and (1) photocopy of passport of accompanying parent
- Original PSA marriage contract of parents or Original Report of Marriage issued by the Philippine Embassy in France (if married in France) of parents, if available.
Important Instruction for applicant:
- Personal appearance of the child and either parent.
- If parents are not married, mother must accompany the minor for renewal.
Certificate of Marriage issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) or Report of Marriage issued by the Philippine Embassy in France (if married in France) is required when only the father files the application. (Original and photocopy)
Certificate of Live Birth/Report of Birth issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA). (Original and photocopy)
Passport of either parent with one (1) photocopy of data page
Additional requirements may be requested by the Embassy in compliance with relevant/prevailing rules and regulations as prescribed by the competent authority.

## Photo Specifications
Passport photograph will be captured at the
                                                                            ePassport Renewal Centre.
Important Reminders:
Eyeglasses, coloured contact lenses,
                                                                            jewellery (earring, necklace), heavy make-up
                                                                            should be removed before photo capturing. It
                                                                            is also advisable for applicants having sore
                                                                            eyes, eye sty or any redness in the eyes to
                                                                            come for e-passport renewal when their eye
                                                                            condition improves.

## Processing Time
The new passport may be released within 4-6 weeks
                                                                        from the date of submission of application,
                                                                        barring unforeseen events

## Application Form
To complete your online application form
                                                                            please
click
                                                                                here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
To complete a manual form please
click
                                                                                here

visa_types.name: ePassport Services
visa_types.overview:
Welcome to the website of the Philippines ePassport Renewal Center (PaRC)
In an effort to ease the ePassport renewal process for Philippines national overseas, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) and VFS Global signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) on the establishment of an ePassport Renewal Center (PaRC).
PaRC aims to provide an alternative means for Filipinos to avail of ePassport renewal services in France.
PaRC is authorized by the DFA to accept ePassport applications from Filipinos holding electronic passports and whose applications do not entail a change in passport record of the holder. The Embassy monitors passport applications lodged through PaRC and the passports that are produced go through the Embassy.
This site contains important information on passport renewal process at PaRC. Please go through the contents carefully before proceeding with your application.
visa_types.full_text:
Welcome to the website of the Philippines ePassport Renewal Center (PaRC)
In an effort to ease the ePassport renewal process for Philippines national overseas, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) and VFS Global signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) on the establishment of an ePassport Renewal Center (PaRC).
PaRC aims to provide an alternative means for Filipinos to avail of ePassport renewal services in France.
PaRC is authorized by the DFA to accept ePassport applications from Filipinos holding electronic passports and whose applications do not entail a change in passport record of the holder. The Embassy monitors passport applications lodged through PaRC and the passports that are produced go through the Embassy.
This site contains important information on passport renewal process at PaRC. Please go through the contents carefully before proceeding with your application.
